original_abstract: This article examines the principles of data acquisition and entry compilation for the crowdsourced dictionary of neologisms developed at the University of Warsaw. The project is situated within the broader landscape of contemporary lexicography, drawing comparisons to existing neologism databases and other community-driven linguistic resources. Furthermore, the author outlines the sociolinguistic factors driving the emergence and adoption of new lexical units while addressing the inherent terminological ambiguity surrounding the concept of the ‘neologism’ itself.
humanized_abstract: The article focuses on the process of data acquisition and data entry of theCrowdictionary project of neologisms at the University of Warsaw. It sets this process against the background of contemporary lexicography, comparing it to other dictionaries of neologisms and referring also to other collective lexis, crowding dictionaries. It discusses sociolinguistic criteria determining the emergence and acceptance of particular words in contemporary language and also presents a number of terms referring to new items in a language vocabulary together with explanations whether they can be regarded as neologisms.
